response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "As a curious man in his sixties who has always been interested in learning, I find myself often caught up in conversations about health topics like COVID vaccines. Living in Milan as a native-born Italian and being married with no children makes these discussions more personal because we're all dealing with the same issues right now.

My low openness score means that when faced with complex scientific debates, I tend to stick to what I know rather than diving into intricate details. However, my curiosity keeps me engaged enough to ask basic questions without getting overwhelmed by technical jargon or statistics from studies. It's important for us ordinary folks like myself who only has a vocational diploma in education level.

Being employed full-time as a sales associate doesn't give much time off work but volunteering is something I enjoy and it helps keep me informed through practical means. My Muslim religious beliefs don't interfere much with health discussions except when there are dietary restrictions or prayer times affecting vaccination schedules, which hasn't been too big an issue yet.

I think managing these conversations involves being patient, asking simple questions that can be understood by everyone, and listening carefully without jumping to conclusions.",

moderate extraversion means comfortable socializing face-to-face or online through simple interactions like asking questions and listening carefully.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a curious man in his sixties who has always been interested in learning, I find myself often caught up in conversations about health topics like COVID vaccines. Living in Milan as a native-born Italian and being married with no children makes these discussions more personal because we're all dealing with the same issues right now.

My low openness score means that when faced with complex scientific debates, I tend to stick to what I know rather than diving into intricate details. However, my curiosity keeps me engaged enough to ask basic questions without getting overwhelmed by technical jargon or statistics from studies. It's important for us ordinary folks like myself who only has a vocational diploma in education level.

Being employed full-time as a sales associate doesn't give much time off work but volunteering is something I enjoy and it helps keep me informed through practical means. My Muslim religious beliefs don't interfere much with health discussions except when there are dietary restrictions or prayer times affecting vaccination schedules, which hasn't been too big an issue yet.

I think managing these conversations involves being patient, asking simple questions that can be understood by everyone, and listening carefully without jumping to conclusions.
